articles.title: Israeli army investigates soldier seen striking Jesus statue in Lebanon
articles.description: IDF says sledgehammer photo circulating online is authentic and is being viewed with ‘great severity’ The Israeli army has said it is investigating a soldier who was seen apparently striking a statue of Jesus with a sledgehammer in southern Lebanon. The Israel Defense Forces said they had determined that an image circulating on social media showing the incident was authentic. The image appears to show an Israeli soldier using a sledgehammer to strike the head of a statue of a crucified Jesus that had fallen off a cross. Continue reading...

articles.title: ‘Flogging’ victim, suspect found dead at Manila condominium
articles.description: MANILA, Philippines — The victim and the suspect in an alleged “flogging” were found dead at a condominium in Tondo, Manila, the police said. The purported incident was discovered by an unnamed witness around 9 p.m. on Sunday, April 19, the Manila Police District (MPD) reported. “[The witness], while inside her room, heard someone screaming for help. Upon checking, she discovered the victim along the hallway. She and other residents attempted to assist, but they observed the suspect inside [the unit]. Out of fear, they immediately left the area,” the MPD explained. READ: BSP employee found dead inside Manila condo [...]... articles.title: Naga, Cebu weekend road crashes: Nursing student, rider killed
articles.description: CEBU CITY, Philippines — Two separate road crashes that happened along the national highway in Naga City, Cebu left two people dead and two others injured over the weekend. One of the fatalities was a 23-year-old nursing student who had just come from a hiking trip before she was killed in a truck collision on Saturday, April 18. Nursing student dies in truck collision Initial investigation showed that two dump trucks were traveling in the same direction — from Toledo City toward Naga City — when the crash occurred at around 1:54 p.m. in Barangay Balirong.
